---
title: "1.8 Documentation with Comments and Preconditions"
subtitle: "Part 3: Use Cases and Precondition Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Preconditions support testing

Preconditions and postconditions help us test code and determine the <span class="term">validity</span> (有效性) of software.

Software validity tests whether the software does what it is supposed to do before it is released.

This can be very important if the code is part of a satellite going to outer space or is going to be used in an emergency condition.

## Good Testers

### Try to break assumptions

Good software testers actually try to break the code.

They try all kinds of input to see what the software will do because you never know what users will try or what conditions there will be.

Always think what the preconditions of your code are, see what happens when you break them, and then see if you can protect or warn against that.

## Use-Case Diagrams

### Designing software systems

Preconditions and postconditions can also help us design better software systems.

Software designers often first draw a high-level <span class="term">Use-Case Diagram</span> of a system that shows the different ways that a user might interact with a system before they build it.

A <span class="term">Use-case</span> is a particular user interaction or situation in the system or software, and use-cases often become methods in the program.

## Restaurant Use-Case Diagram {.image-fit}

### Customer, staff, and use-cases

![Use-Case Diagram of a Restaurant System](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-8-documentation-with-comments/use-case-restaurant.png){fig-align="center" width="64%"}

The diagram shows `2` actors in the system: the customer and the staff at the restaurant, and `3` use-cases in circles.

## Use-Case Conditions

### Order Food

After drawing a Use-Case Diagram, designers write down the preconditions and the postconditions for each Use-Case.

Often the successful post-condition for one use-case becomes the preconditions for the next use-case.

::: {.tight-list}
- Preconditions for "Order Food": Customer enters restaurant. Staff is ready to take the order.
- Postconditions for "Order Food": Customer orders the food. Staff takes the order.
:::

## Use-Case Conditions

### Eat Food

::: {.tight-list}
- Preconditions for "Eat Food": Customer has already ordered food. Staff has delivered food.
- Postcondition for "Eat Food": Customer eats the food.
:::

## Student Response Task

### `shortanswer:: payconditions`

What are the preconditions and postconditions of the use-case "Pay for food"?

Remember that these are often related to the other use-case conditions "order food" and "eat food".

## Groupwork

### Coding Challenge: Preconditions in Algorithms

Working in pairs or groups, come up with `4` steps that a user must do to purchase a product, for example a book on Java, in an online store.

List the preconditions and postconditions for each step.

You could pretend to buy something online to come up with the steps.

Do not forget to list the preconditions and postconditions for each step.

## Student Response Task

### `shortanswer:: challenge-1-8-use-case-preconditions`

Write down `4` steps that a user must do to purchase a product, for example a book on Java, in an online store.

For each step, list:

::: {.tight-list}
- the use-case step
- the preconditions
- the postconditions
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that software validity asks whether the software does what it is supposed to do
- describe why testers try to break preconditions
- identify actors and use-cases in the restaurant diagram
- connect "Order Food", "Eat Food", and "Pay for food" with pre/postconditions
- write `4` online-store purchase steps
- list preconditions and postconditions for each online-store step
:::

## End

### 1.8 complete

The next topic begins method signatures.
